_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot)
_meta.note: Houston, TX local batch — evidence-based finalization of 4 City of Houston council members (Pollard D-J, Flickinger R-E, Martinez D-I, Ramirez R-AtLarge1). Roster verified current June 2026 (all terms run to Jan 2, 2028). HPD Chief J. Noe Diaz Jr. was researched but DEFERRED this batch: the shared office-tier classifier (refine-records.py + generate-profiles.py) maps 'Police Chief' to state tier, which would publicly mislabel a city police chief — flagged to Adam to add 'police chief|chief of police' -> local before finalizing him. Scores reset to null where no public evidence; FALSE only where a documented vote/statement/funding action exists; TRUE where documented affirmative alignment. Many cells null because a municipal office rarely touches life/marriage/guns/elections directly — silence is honestly null, not penalized.

profile.religion: Antioch Missionary Baptist Church, Houston (lifetime member; grandson of Rev. Dr. T.J. Jemison)
profile.education: Morehouse College (BA Political Science); Thurgood Marshall School of Law (JD); Harvard Business School (cert)
profile.background: City of Houston Council Member, District J (assumed office Jan 2020; re-elected Nov 2023, term-limited, term ends Jan 2, 2028). Houston civil-litigation attorney (Pollard Legal Group). Characterized as a centrist/more conservative Democrat.

edward-pollard.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Roster verified: District J, term-limited, term ends Jan 2, 2028; lifetime member Antioch Missionary Baptist Church. HARD evidence: cast the SOLE 'no' vote against Mayor Whitmire's ~$7.5B FY2027 budget (June 2026), saying 'A budget is discipline... my conscience can't allow me to spend the people's purse with any less discipline than I would my own' and warning of the 'largest budget deficits ever' (economic_stewardship TRUE — genuine fiscal hawk). Affirmative pro-police record: created the 'District J Patrol' community-policing program and a Police Transparency Dashboard, stating 'I fully support our first responders. Public safety is paramount' (public_justice q0 TRUE). Counter-direction: co-sponsored/pushed an ordinance LIMITING HPD-ICE cooperation (barring HPD from contacting ICE on civil warrants), later reversed under TX SB4/Gov. Abbott pressure (border_immigration q1, q2 FALSE). A Democrat with a hard-evidenced fiscal-conservative + pro-police record but conventionally progressive on immigration. NO EVIDENCE found on life, marriage, LGBTQ, schools, Christian liberty, election integrity, state-overreach, or guns — those cells null.

2.note: Sole 'no' vote against the ~$7.5B FY2027 budget (June 2026); warned of record deficits and called the plan fiscally undisciplined.

0.note: Affirmative pro-police advocacy: created the 'District J Patrol' community-policing program and a Police Transparency Dashboard; 'I fully support our first responders. Public safety is paramount.'

1.note: Co-sponsored a measure to LIMIT HPD-ICE cooperation; said HPD 'should solely focus on local matters' and sought to bar HPD from contacting ICE on civil warrants.

2.note: Pushed a pro-sanctuary-direction ordinance (remove ICE-contact requirement, bar detention on civil warrants); city later forced to reverse under TX SB4/Abbott pressure.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic — active parishioner, St. Martha Catholic Church, Houston
profile.background: City of Houston Council Member, District E (elected Nov 2023, sworn in Jan 2024, term ends Jan 2, 2028). Owner, Flickinger Machine (family business); former logistics-corp executive. Kingwood TEA Party board, Lake Houston Pachyderm Club president, Harris County GOP Chairman's Circle. Endorsed by Republican Party of Texas, Conservative Republicans of Texas, True Texas Project, Houston police-officer org.

fred-flickinger.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Roster verified: District E, term ends Jan 2, 2028; active Roman Catholic (St. Martha). HARD evidence: opposed a proposed property-tax-rate increase and offered budget amendments to cut spending (pension opt-out, ending the 1.75% civic-art set-aside), saying 'the only direction the government goes is to grow and grow and grow, requiring more and more revenue' (economic_stewardship TRUE). Affirmative pro-police: police-officer-org endorsement, 'supporting our first responders' as a stated priority, pushed HPD hiring/community-policing funding, and co-signed a dissent defending officers against the ICE-limit ordinance (public_justice q0 TRUE). Voted AGAINST the April 2026 ordinance limiting HPD-ICE cooperation and co-signed a dissent warning of SB4 conflict (border_immigration q2 TRUE — opposes sanctuary-style policy). Consistently conservative; no party deviation found. Social-conservative cells (life/marriage/family/Christian-liberty/guns/elections) are NO EVIDENCE — silence given the municipal scope, not opposition — so null.

2.note: Opposed a proposed property-tax-rate hike and offered spending-cut amendments (pension opt-out, ending the 1.75% art requirement); 'government goes is to grow and grow... requiring more and more revenue.'

0.note: Affirmative pro-police advocacy: police-org endorsement, 'supporting our first responders' priority, pushed HPD hiring; co-signed dissent defending officers in the ICE vote.

2.note: Voted AGAINST the April 8, 2026 ordinance limiting HPD-ICE cooperation; co-signed a joint dissent warning of SB4 conflict — opposed the sanctuary-style policy.

profile.religion: Raised Catholic (served as an altar boy; no denominational confirmation beyond bio)
profile.education: BA Political Science, University of Houston
profile.background: City of Houston Council Member, District I (East End) — won the open seat Nov 2023, sworn in Jan 2024, term ends Jan 2, 2028. Previously ~9 yrs Director of Community Affairs for District I; prior nonprofit work (BakerRipley, Tejano Center).

joaquin-martinez.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Roster verified: District I, term ends Jan 2, 2028; raised Catholic (altar boy). HARD evidence: one of four council members who proposed a ~6% property-tax-rate increase (to ~$0.55/$100) in Oct 2024, against the mayor's no-change plan (economic_stewardship FALSE). Pro-immigrant / sanctuary-leaning: 'immigrants are Houstonians... I will continue to stand with immigrant families' and voted FOR the April 2026 ordinance limiting HPD-ICE cooperation (removing the 30-minute ICE window, barring prolonged stops on civil warrants) (border_immigration q1, q2 FALSE). His later vote for an amendment was explicitly to preserve $110M+ in state funding ('responsible governance') while reaffirming support for immigrant families — fiscal pragmatism, not an enforcement stance. 'Community policing' framing does not meet the affirmative pro-police bar → public_justice q0 null. Aligns with party expectation; no favorable deviation found. NO EVIDENCE on life/marriage/family/Christian-liberty/elections/state-overreach/guns — null.

2.note: One of four council members who proposed a ~6% property-tax-rate increase (to ~$0.55/$100) in Oct 2024, opposing the mayor's no-change plan.

1.note: 'Immigrants are Houstonians... I will continue to stand with immigrant families... policies grounded in fairness, transparency and dignity' — opposes mandatory deportation.

2.note: Voted FOR the April 8, 2026 ordinance limiting HPD-ICE cooperation (removed the 30-minute ICE window, barred prolonging stops on civil warrants) — sanctuary-leaning.

profile.religion: Latter-day Saint (LDS) — active member, Memorial Park First Ward, Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints
profile.education: BA (1986) and JD (1989), University of Texas at Austin
profile.background: City of Houston Council Member, At-Large Position 1 (sworn in Jan 2024, term ends Jan 2, 2028). Republican. Career prosecutor — Harris County Assistant District Attorney 1989-2016 (chief felony prosecutor; led public-corruption and civil-rights divisions). Ran for judge of the 248th Criminal District Court as GOP nominee in 2022 and LOST (was never a sitting judge).

julian-ramirez.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Roster verified/corrected: At-Large Position 1, term ends Jan 2, 2028; LDS (Memorial Park First Ward). IMPORTANT CORRECTION — he was a career PROSECUTOR (Harris Co. ADA 1989-2016), NOT a judge; he ran for the 248th Criminal District Court in 2022 and lost. HARD evidence of party deviation: used his own council budget to help fund the city's LGBTQ+ Economic Empowerment Coordinator (announced Oct 28, 2025) (biblical_marriage q4 FALSE); publicly supported and voted for the April 2026 ordinance curbing HPD-ICE coordination (passed 12-5), defending it as 'about good government' and criticizing ICE's administrative-warrant 'dragnet' (border_immigration q2 FALSE). Despite a prosecutor pedigree, his council criminal-justice profile is oversight/accountability-leaning (delayed a $14M county payment over jail deaths) rather than punitive, and budget votes don't meet the affirmative pro-police bar → public_justice cells null. FY2025 budget vote carried no tax-rate hike and no fiscal-hawk advocacy → economic_stewardship null. NO EVIDENCE on life/family/Christian-liberty/elections/state-overreach/guns — null.

4.note: Used his own council budget to help fund the city's LGBTQ+ Economic Empowerment Coordinator (Oct 2025) — affirmatively supported promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy.

2.note: Supported and voted for the April 2026 ordinance limiting HPD-ICE coordination (passed 12-5), defending it and criticizing ICE's administrative-warrant 'dragnet.'
